‚Äã

**THE POWER OF ROUTINE**

I was on a podcast the other day, on time management. And was asked this interesting question.  
"You seem to be a person of routine. However, does a routine leave space for serendipity, luck or just casual thinking? When you are so set in your calendar, are you not a slave to the calendar itself?"

It's a brilliant question.   
It seems that the world offers two really poor choices.   
Choice 1 is to not have a routine. But then that makes us feel guilty of managing our time effectively, procrastinating and ultimately not accomplishing the things we wanted to.  
Choice 2 is to have a routine. And then lead such a boring life that you look like a robot, moving from one task to another!

These seemingly poor choices emerge because of a lack of understanding of what comprises a routine.   
A routine is NOT moving from one task to another.   
A routine is doing something repeatedly to the point it becomes natural to your life.  
Which means, it can very well be a routine to 'do nothing'.

Let me explain, using my life as an example.

I have a morning routine, that I love.   
I wake up, meditate, read, get the kids ready for school, play tennis, go to the gym, get ready, have a nice breakfast.   
This is the most important part of my day.   
That is my routine.   
However when I sit down for my work, I have a very different routine.   
That routine is to spend 1-2 hours everyday with NO AGENDA.   
I read online, browse twitter, write on paper, reflect, look out the window, take breaks.   
And that is ALSO a routine.   
And then during the day, I have my distraction time scheduled.   
Time that I have scheduled to 'waste time' (mostly on social media, mostly IG reels, haha).  
That is ALSO a routine.   
I end my day with an early meal, spending time with family, reading again and sleeping early.  
That is ALSO a routine.

So within a day, I have 3 forms of routine.  
1. A set routine comprising tasks.  
2. A fluid routine comprising thinking and reflecting.  
3. A routine specific to wasting time.

Whether you are a student, a working professional, a homemaker, a freelancer, an entrepreneur - a routine is critical.   
How you structure the routine is specific to your current context.   
Not having one though, is a choice! And a poor one at that, if I may add.

This one thing beats everything else.   
It beats money, it beats talent, it even beats luck!  
Because a routine gives you all of it.

**A pro is the amateur that showed up everyday!**

‚Äã

**BOOK I AM READING THIS WEEK**

Currently reading [The YouTube Formula: How Anyone Can Unlock the Algorithm to Drive Views, Build an Audience, and Grow Revenue](https://amzn.to/3AfENKo)‚Äã

This book by Derral Eves goes into the details of how YouTube as a platform works for creators. Derral has worked closely with Mr. Beast and understands YouTube better than most people in the world!   
Really nice simple detailed book, if you care about mastering the YT algorithm.

**RESPONSES TO LAST WEEK'S QUESTION**

Last week I asked you:

Will you be watching the T20 Cricket WC Final?

1. Yes
2. No
3. T20 WC is going on??

Here are the responses:

[![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!A0tQ!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F581b7389-56dc-40a1-802d-7dfd3c3a8197_1059x275.png)](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!A0tQ!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F581b7389-56dc-40a1-802d-7dfd3c3a8197_1059x275.png)

‚Äã

Observations:

* Interesting to see how the younger population isn't either interested or even aware of the T20 happening.
* The older population is more keen to watch it.

My response?  
I didn't intend to watch the final. I saw the India-Pak match (a cricket match after some 5 years I think) and that was enough! What a match!

**QUESTION OF THE WEEK**

Which of these is the best part about your life right now?

1. Your job/studies
2. Your family
3. Your friends
4. Your love life
5. Nothing is good about my life right now
